name: Thailand Cybersecurity Act B.E. 2562 (2019)
summary: Thailand's Cybersecurity Act establishes the National Cybersecurity Committee (NCSC) and a legal framework for preventing, handling, and responding to cybersecurity threats. It designates Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) across government and private sectors and grants authorities emergency powers during severe cyber threats.
keyRequirements: National Cybersecurity Committee (NCSC) chaired by the Prime Minister for policy coordination
Designation of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) organizations across key sectors
CII organizations must maintain cybersecurity standards and conduct risk assessments
Mandatory cybersecurity incident reporting to the NCSC
Emergency powers allowing authorities to access computer systems during critical-level cyber threats
Thai-CERT coordination for national incident response and threat intelligence sharing
penalties: Fines up to THB 300,000 for CII organizations failing to comply with cybersecurity standards. Imprisonment up to 3 years and/or fines up to THB 60,000 for obstructing NCSC operations. Additional penalties for failure to report cybersecurity incidents.
